speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Your wondrous rare description, noble earl,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Of beauteous Margaret hath astonished me.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Her virtues gracèd with external gifts

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Do breed love’s settled passions in my heart,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And like as rigor of tempestuous gusts

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Provokes the mightiest hulk against the tide,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: So am I driven by breath of her renown

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Either to suffer shipwrack, or arrive

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Where I may have fruition of her love.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Tush, my good lord, this superficial tale

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Is but a preface of her worthy praise.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: The chief perfections of that lovely dame,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Had I sufficient skill to utter them,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Would make a volume of enticing lines

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Able to ravish any dull conceit;

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: And, which is more, she is not so divine,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: So full replete with choice of all delights,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: But with as humble lowliness of mind

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: She is content to be at your command—

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Command, I mean, of virtuous chaste intents—

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: To love and honor Henry as her lord.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And otherwise will Henry ne’er presume.—

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Therefore, my Lord Protector, give consent

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: That Margaret may be England’s royal queen.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: So should I give consent to flatter sin.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: You know, my lord, your Highness is betrothed

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Unto another lady of esteem.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: How shall we then dispense with that contract

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: And not deface your honor with reproach?

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: As doth a ruler with unlawful oaths;

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Or one that, at a triumph having vowed

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: To try his strength, forsaketh yet the lists

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: By reason of his adversary’s odds.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: A poor earl’s daughter is unequal odds,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: And therefore may be broke without offense.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Why, what, I pray, is Margaret more than that?

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Her father is no better than an earl,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Although in glorious titles he excel.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Yes, my lord, her father is a king,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: The King of Naples and Jerusalem,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: And of such great authority in France

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: As his alliance will confirm our peace,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: And keep the Frenchmen in allegiance.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: And so the Earl of Armagnac may do,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Because he is near kinsman unto Charles.

speaker: EXETER
text: Besides, his wealth doth warrant a liberal dower,

speaker: EXETER
text: Where Reignier sooner will receive than give.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: A dower, my lords? Disgrace not so your king

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: That he should be so abject, base, and poor,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: To choose for wealth and not for perfect love.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Henry is able to enrich his queen,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: And not to seek a queen to make him rich;

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: So worthless peasants bargain for their wives,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: As market men for oxen, sheep, or horse.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Marriage is a matter of more worth

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Than to be dealt in by attorneyship.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Not whom we will, but whom his Grace affects,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Must be companion of his nuptial bed.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: And therefore, lords, since he affects her most,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Most of all these reasons bindeth us

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: In our opinions she should be preferred.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: For what is wedlock forcèd but a hell,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: An age of discord and continual strife?

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Whereas the contrary bringeth bliss

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: And is a pattern of celestial peace.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Whom should we match with Henry, being a king,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: But Margaret, that is daughter to a king?

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Her peerless feature, joinèd with her birth,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Approves her fit for none but for a king.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Her valiant courage and undaunted spirit,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: More than in women commonly is seen,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Will answer our hope in issue of a king.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: For Henry, son unto a conqueror,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Is likely to beget more conquerors,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: If with a lady of so high resolve

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: As is fair Margaret he be linked in love.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Then yield, my lords, and here conclude with me

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: That Margaret shall be queen, and none but she.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Whether it be through force of your report,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: My noble Lord of Suffolk, or for that

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: My tender youth was never yet attaint

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: With any passion of inflaming love,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: I cannot tell; but this I am assured:

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: I feel such sharp dissension in my breast,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Such fierce alarums both of hope and fear,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: As I am sick with working of my thoughts.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Take therefore shipping; post, my lord, to France;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Agree to any covenants, and procure

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: That Lady Margaret do vouchsafe to come

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: To cross the seas to England and be crowned

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: King Henry’s faithful and anointed queen.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: For your expenses and sufficient charge,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Among the people gather up a tenth.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Be gone, I say, for till you do return,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: I rest perplexèd with a thousand cares.—

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And you, good uncle, banish all offense.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: If you do censure me by what you were,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Not what you are, I know it will excuse

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: This sudden execution of my will.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And so conduct me where, from company,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: I may revolve and ruminate my grief.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Ay, grief, I fear me, both at first and last.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Thus Suffolk hath prevailed, and thus he goes

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: As did the youthful Paris once to Greece,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: With hope to find the like event in love,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: But prosper better than the Trojan did.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Margaret shall now be queen, and rule the King,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: But I will rule both her, the King, and realm.
